name: Southern Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 475°F.
2. Lightly grease a baking sheet.
3. In a large mixing bowl, make a well in the center of the sifted flour.
4. Add the lard and buttermilk to the flour mixture.
5. Using your fingers, work the flour into the milk and shortening until a soft dough forms. This will be a sticky dough.
6. Remove the dough from the bowl and gently fold, knead, or press with your fingertips, turning the dough until it is no longer sticky.
7. Pat the dough to a thickness of about 1/2 inch.
8. Cut the dough into rounds using a 2-inch biscuit cutter (or pinch off small portions of dough about 1 1/2 inches in diameter, roll them in your palms, and press with knuckles to flatten slightly).
9. Place the biscuits on the prepared baking sheet. For crispy outside biscuits, space them about 1 inch apart. For softer biscuits, place them with edges touching.
10. Bake for about 8 minutes, until lightly browned. Serve hot from the oven with butter if desired.
